5: export const readOptionalFromRoot = async (

8:   try {
9:     return await readFileFromRoot(relativePath);
10:   } catch {
11:     return null;
12:   }
13: };
14: 
15: export const getRegistryUiSourceCandidates = ({ name }: { name: string }) => [
16:   path.join("registry", "new-york", `${name}.tsx`),
17: ];
18: 
19: export const getDemoSource = (name: string): Promise<string | null> =>
20:   readOptionalFromRoot(path.join("examples", `${name}.tsx`));
21: 
22: export const getRegistrySource = async (
23:   name: string
24: ): Promise<string | null> => {
25:   const candidates = getRegistryUiSourceCandidates({ name });
26: 
27:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
28:     const code = await readOptionalFromRoot(candidate);
29:     if (code) {
30:       return code;
31:     }
32:   }
33: 
34:   return null;
35: };

import { ChevronDown } from "lucide-react";
import { motion, useReducedMotion } from "motion/react";
import type { Transition } from "motion/react";
import { useCallback, useId, useLayoutEffect, useRef, useState } from "react";
import type { ReactNode } from "react";

import { EASE_OUT } from "@/lib/ease";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

export interface AccordionItem {
  id: string;
  title: ReactNode;
  description?: ReactNode;
  icon?: ReactNode;
  disabled?: boolean;
}

export interface AccordionClassNames {
  root?: string;
  item?: string;
  trigger?: string;
  icon?: string;
  title?: string;
  chevron?: string;
  content?: string;
  description?: string;
}

export interface AccordionProps {
  items: AccordionItem[];
  value?: string | null;
  defaultValue?: string | null;
  onValueChange?: (value: string | null) => void;
  collapsible?: boolean;
  className?: string;
  classNames?: AccordionClassNames;
}

// Local springs keep the accordion's connected groups moving together while
// avoiding scale projection on text-heavy row contents.
// Gap spring: must not overshoot y — positive y overshoot drifts items below
// their mt-3 resting point and briefly overlaps the next item.
const ROW_TRANSITION: Transition = {
  bounce: 0.18,
  duration: 0.45,
  type: "spring",
};

const CONTENT_OPEN_TRANSITION: Transition = {
  bounce: 0.16,
  duration: 0.46,
  type: "spring",
};

const CONTENT_CLOSE_TRANSITION: Transition = {
  bounce: 0.14,
  duration: 0.4,
  type: "spring",
};

const DESCRIPTION_TRANSITION: Transition = {
  duration: 0.22,
  ease: EASE_OUT,
};

const CHEVRON_TRANSITION: Transition = {
  bounce: 0.16,
  duration: 0.38,
  type: "spring",
};

const useControllableAccordionValue = function useControllableAccordionValue({
  value,
  defaultValue,
  onValueChange,
}: {
  value?: string | null;
  defaultValue?: string | nu... (line truncated to 2000 chars)
14:       "type": "registry:ui",

16:     },
17:     {
18:       "path": "lib/ease.ts",
19:       "content": "// Shared motion tokens. Easing curves mirror the CSS custom properties in
// globals.css; springs are the canonical physics used across components.
// Strong custom variants — defaults like `ease-in`/`ease-out` feel weak.

export const EASE_OUT = [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1] as const;
export const EASE_IN_OUT = [0.77, 0, 0.175, 1] as const;
export const EASE_DRAWER = [0.32, 0.72, 0, 1] as const;

/** CSS string form of EASE_OUT for inline style transitions. */
export const EASE_OUT_CSS = "cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)";

/** Press feedback on buttons and other tappable surfaces. */
export const SPRING_PRESS = {
  damping: 30,
  mass: 0.6,
  stiffness: 500,
  type: "spring",
} as const;

/** Content swaps — label/icon slots trading places inside a control. */
export const SPRING_SWAP = {
  damping: 30,
  mass: 0.55,
  stiffness: 460,
  type: "spring",
} as const;

/** Overlay panel entrances — modals and sheets summoned by pointer. */
export const SPRING_PANEL = {
  damping: 40,
  mass: 0.5,
  stiffness: 420,
  type: "spring",
} as const;

/** Shared-layout glides — pills, indicators and panels morphing between positions. */
export const SPRING_LAYOUT = {
  damping: 32,
  mass: 0.6,
  stiffness: 360,
  type: "spring",
} as const;

/** Cursor-follow physics for decorative mouse tracking (magnetic, tilt, dock). */
export const SPRING_MOUSE = {
  damping: 15,
  mass: 0.3,
  stiffness: 200,
} as const;

<path>/home/himmel/personal/prsui/content/docs/components/accordion.mdx</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: ---
2: title: Accordion
3: description: Animated accordion with spring physics, group morphing, and accessible ARIA attributes.
4: ---
5: 
6: import { BouncyAccordionPreview } from "@/demo/accordion-demo";
7: 
8: <ComponentPreview name="accordion">
9:   <BouncyAccordionPreview />
10: </ComponentPreview>
11: 
12: ## Installation
13: 
14: If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:
15: 
16: <CodeBlockCommand
17:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
18:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
19:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
20:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/accordion"
21: />
22: 
23: Otherwise, use the full registry URL:
24: 
25: <CodeBlockCommand
26:   __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
27:   __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
28:   __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
29:   __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/accordion.json"
30: />
31: 
32: ## Usage
33: 
34: After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:
35: 
36: ```tsx
37: import { Accordion, type AccordionItem } from "@/components/ui/accordion";
38: 
39: const items: AccordionItem[] = [
40:   {
41:     id: "item-1",
42:     title: "First question",
43:     description: "Answer to the first question.",
44:   },
45:   {
46:     id: "item-2",
47:     title: "Second question",
48:     description: "Answer to the second question.",
49:   },
50: ];
51: 
52: export function MyComponent() {
53:   return <Accordion items={items} defaultValue="item-1" />;
54: }
55: ```
56: 
57: ## Props
58: 
59: | Prop            | Type                              | Default | Description                                           |
60: | --------------- | --------------------------------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
61: | `items`         | `AccordionItem[]`                 | -       | Array of accordion items                              |
62: | `value`         | `string \| null`                  | -       | Controlled currently open item id                     |
63: | `defaultValue`  | `string \| null`                  | `null`  | Initially open item id                                |
64: | `onValueChange` | `(value: string \| null) => void` | -       | Callback fired when the open item changes             |
65: | `collapsible`   | `boolean`                         | `true`  | When false, an open item cannot be closed by clicking |
66: | `className`     | `string`                          | -       | Classes applied to the root wrapper                   |
67: | `classNames`    | `AccordionClassNames`             | -       | Override classes for individual sub-elements          |
68: 
69: ### AccordionItem
70: 
71: | Field         | Type        | Description                              |
72: | ------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------- |
73: | `id`          | `string`    | Unique identifier for the item           |
74: | `title`       | `ReactNode` | Content shown in the trigger row         |
75: | `description` | `ReactNode` | Content revealed when the item is open   |
76: | `icon`        | `ReactNode` | Optional icon displayed before the title |
77: | `disabled`    | `boolean`   | Disables the item                        |
78: 
79: ### AccordionClassNames
80: 
81: | Key           | Description                  |
82: | ------------- | ---------------------------- |
83: | `root`        | Wrapper around all items     |
84: | `item`        | Each accordion row container |
85: | `trigger`     | The clickable button row     |
86: | `icon`        | The icon wrapper span        |
87: | `title`       | The title text span          |
88: | `chevron`     | The chevron rotation wrapper |
89: | `content`     | The animated content wrapper |
90: | `description` | The description text div     |

(End of file - total 90 lines)
</content>

input.content:
"use client"

import { RotateCw } from "lucide-react"

import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"

export function RestartButton({ onRestart }: { onRestart: () => void }) {
  return (
      <Button
        variant="outline"
        size="icon"
        onClick={onRestart}
        className="h-8 w-8 active:scale-95 duration-300 ease-out transition-[scale,background-color,opacity] group/restart-button"
        aria-label="Restart demo"
      >
        <RotateCw
          className="h-4 w-4 group-hover/restart-button:rotate-45 duration-300 ease-out transition-transform"
        />
      </Button>
  )
}

result.output: Wrote file successfully.

28:           node.properties.__bun__ = raw.replace("npx", "bunx --bun");
29:         } else if (raw.startsWith("npm create")) {
30:           node.properties.__npm__ = raw;
31:           node.properties.__yarn__ = raw.replace("npm create", "yarn create");
32:           node.properties.__pnpm__ = raw.replace("npm create", "pnpm create");
33:           node.properties.__bun__ = raw.replace("npm create", "bun create");
34:         } else if (raw.startsWith("npx")) {
35:           node.properties.__npm__ = raw;
36:           node.properties.__yarn__ = raw.replace("npx", "yarn dlx");
37:           node.properties.__pnpm__ = raw.replace("npx", "pnpm dlx");
38:           node.properties.__bun__ = raw.replace("npx", "bunx --bun");
39:         } else if (raw.startsWith("npm run")) {
40:           node.properties.__npm__ = raw;
41:           node.properties.__yarn__ = raw.replace("npm run", "yarn");
42:           node.properties.__pnpm__ = raw.replace("npm run", "pnpm");
43:           node.properties.__bun__ = raw.replace("npm run", "bun");
44:         }
45:       }
46:     },
47:   },
48: ] as ShikiTransformer[];
49: 
50: export const highlightCode = async (code: string, language = "tsx") => {
51:   const cacheKey = createHash("sha256")
52:     .update(`${language}:${code}`)
53:     .digest("hex");
54: 
55:   const cached = highlightCache.get(cacheKey);
56:   if (cached) {
57:     return cached;
58:   }
59: 
60:   const html = await codeToHtml(code, {
61:     lang: language,
62:     themes: {
63:       dark: "github-dark",
64:       light: "github-light",
65:     },
66:     transformers: [
67:       {
68:         code(node) {
69:           node.properties["data-line-numbers"] = "";
70:         },
71:         line(node) {
72:           node.properties["data-line"] = "";
73:         },
74:         pre(node) {
75:           node.properties.class =
76:             "no-scrollbar min-w-0 overflow-x-auto px-4 py-3.5 outline-none has-[[data-highlighted-line]]:px-0 has-[[data-line-numbers]]:px-0 has-[[data-slot=tabs]]:p-0 !bg-transparent";
77:         },
78:       },
79:     ],
80:   });
81: 
82:   highlightCache.set(cacheKey, html);
83: 
84:   return html;
85: };

(End of file - total 85 lines)

2: import { Project, ScriptKind } from "ts-morph";
3: import type { SourceFile } from "ts-morph";
4: 
5: const buildDisplayConfig = () => ({
6:   $schema: "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json",
7:   aliases: {
8:     components: "@/components",
9:     hooks: "@/hooks",
10:     lib: "@/lib",
11:     ui: "@/components/ui",
12:     utils: "@/lib/utils",
13:   },
14:   iconLibrary: "lucide",
15:   resolvedPaths: {
16:     components: "@/components",

18:     hooks: "@/hooks",
19:     lib: "@/lib",
20:     tailwindConfig: "",
21:     tailwindCss: "",
22:     ui: "@/components/ui",
23:     utils: "@/lib/utils",
24:   },
25:   rsc: true,
26:   style: "base-nova",
27:   tailwind: {
28:     baseColor: "neutral",
29:     config: "",
30:     css: "",
31:     cssVariables: true,
32:     prefix: "",
33:   },
34:   tsx: true,
35: });
36: 
37: type DisplayTransformer = (opts: {
38:   filename: string;
39:   raw: string;
40:   sourceFile: SourceFile;
41:   config: ReturnType<typeof buildDisplayConfig>;
42: }) => Promise<unknown>;
43: 
44: export const formatCode = async (code: string) => {
45:   let formattedCode = code;
46: 
47:   formattedCode = formattedCode.replaceAll(
48:     "@/registry/new-york/",
49:     "@/components/"
50:   );
51: 
52:   formattedCode = formattedCode.replaceAll("export default", "export");
53: 
54:   try {
55:     const config = buildDisplayConfig();
56:     const project = new Project({ compilerOptions: {} });
57:     const sourceFile = project.createSourceFile(
58:       "component.tsx",
59:       formattedCode,
60:       {
61:         scriptKind: ScriptKind.TSX,
62:       }
63:     );
64: 
65:     const transformers: DisplayTransformer[] = [
66:       transformIcons as DisplayTransformer,
67:       transformMenu as DisplayTransformer,
68:       transformRender as DisplayTransformer,
69:     ];
70: 
71:     await Promise.all(
72:       transformers.map((transformer) =>
73:         transformer({
74:           config,
75:           filename: "component.tsx",
76:           raw: formattedCode,
77:           sourceFile,
78:         })
79:       )
80:     );
81: 
82:     return sourceFile.getText().trim();
83:   } catch (error) {
84:     console.error("Transform failed:", error);
85:     return code;
86:   }
87: };

1: import { track } from "@vercel/analytics";
2: import { z } from "zod";
3: 
4: const eventSchema = z.object({
5:   name: z.enum([
6:     "copy_npm_command",
7:     "copy_usage_import_code",
8:     "copy_usage_code",
9:     "copy_primitive_code",
10:     "copy_theme_code",
11:     "copy_block_code",
12:     "copy_chunk_code",
13:     "enable_lift_mode",
14:     "copy_chart_code",
15:     "copy_chart_theme",
16:     "copy_chart_data",
17:     "copy_color",
18:     "set_layout",
19:     "open_command_menu",
20:     "click_edit_page",
21:     "click_registry_add_button",
22:     "copy_registry_command",
23:     "keyboard_shortcut_navigate",
24:   ]),
25:   properties: z
26:     .record(
27:       z.string(),
28:       z.union([z.string(), z.number(), z.boolean(), z.null()])
29:     )
30:     .optional(),
31: });
32: 
33: export type Event = z.infer<typeof eventSchema>;
34: 
35: export const trackEvent = (input: Event): void => {
36:   const event = eventSchema.parse(input);
37:   if (event) {
38:     track(event.name, event.properties);
39:   }
40: };
